{"problem_standardized_math":"The Cauchy--Schwarz inequality states that for any vectors $u$ and $v$,\n\\[\n|u^T v|^2 \\le (u^T u)(v^T v),\n\\]\nwith equality only when $u$ and $v$ are parallel. If $B$ is positive definite, use this inequality to show that\n\\[\n\\gamma \\stackrel{\\mathrm{def}}{=} \\frac{\\|g\\|^4}{(g^TBg)(g^T B^{-1}g)} \\le 1,\n\\]\nwith equality only if $g$ and $Bg$ (and hence $B^{-1}g$) are parallel."}
